title: Private VPC and routes
content: Place workloads in private subnets, route only required traffic through explicit route tables, and keep public entry separate from service subnets.

title: Security group relationships
content: Express service-to-service access with source security group rules, not broad public CIDRs. Permit only protocol and port required by the receiving workload.

title: CloudFront private ALB origin
content: CloudFront is the only public product entry. Keep the application load balancer private and constrain origin traffic with the CloudFront managed prefix list.

title: EKS managed nodes and IRSA
content: Use an EKS managed node group for private backend capacity and assign workload AWS permissions through a narrowly scoped IRSA role rather than node-wide or static credentials.

title: RDS network boundary
content: Associate RDS with a database subnet group built from private subnets and authorize database traffic only from the application security group.

title: S3 content and RDB metadata
content: Store uploaded object content in S3 while the relational database owns metadata, ownership, and lifecycle state. Do not use the corpus bucket for uploads, generated Terraform, state, or secrets.

title: SQS asynchronous events
content: Use SQS for asynchronous progress or result events and grant producers only send permissions to the specific queue required by the backend workflow.

title: GitHub OIDC and IRSA trust
content: Federated roles must require the STS audience and an exact trusted subject. Scope GitHub ingestion to its named environment and scope Kubernetes trust to one service account.

title: Private OpenSearch Serverless collection
content: Use one classic VECTORSEARCH collection with AWS-owned encryption and disabled standby replicas. Network policy permits only its VPC endpoint; do not add public or Dashboards access.

title: AOSS reader and ingester separation
content: The backend reader may describe the target index and search documents. A separate OIDC ingester may create or update that index and write documents; neither principal manages collection security policies.

title: Common tags and safe outputs
content: Apply explicit project, environment, manager, and component tags. Export collection and contract values as Terraform outputs without committing runtime identifiers.

title: Immutable image and secret guardrails
content: Deploy backend images by immutable digest. Keep credentials and secrets outside generated configuration, corpus chunks, logs, and source control.
